subject: Chemistry
title: Discovery of Acids
description: A micro-lesson that makes Discovery of Acids usable.

hook.text: Alchemists discovered the three mineral acids—sulfuric, hydrochloric, and nitric—that made modern chemistry possible.

buildup.text: Sulfuric acid (oil of vitriol) was made by heating green vitriol (iron sulfate). Jabir described it in the 8th century.

discovery.text: Hydrochloric acid (spirit of salt) came from heating salt with vitriol. Nitric acid came from saltpeter and vitriol.

twist.text: These acids could dissolve metals, separate compounds, and create new substances—powers no previous chemical had.

climax.text: Without mineral acids, there would be no fertilizers, no explosives, no steel, no plastics. Industrial chemistry depends on them.

punchline.text: Three acids that dissolve metals and build industries. Alchemy's most useful legacy.

quiz.question: What were the three mineral acids discovered by alchemists?
